description: Configurable civic and research buildings for the Wenyu Valley standalone viewport. Buildings are ordered along the road network and can be edited without changing MoonBit code.

modules.title: Town Shell
modules.use_case: Mayor supervision, runtime projection, town-wide orchestration, and operator dispatch.
modules.agent_flow: Agents report status here, receive mayor routing decisions, then return to their home books or civic buildings.
modules.summary: Mayor supervision, runtime projection, and whole-town operator control.

modules.title: Policy Hall
modules.use_case: AI policy triage, eligibility checks, cited explanations, and application checklists.
modules.agent_flow: Policy agents enter with resident questions, triage evidence, draft reviewable checklists, then return accepted updates to the policy MoonBook.
modules.summary: Policy quests, eligibility checks, cited explanations, and application checklists.

modules.title: Vitality Tower
modules.use_case: Town pulse, service response, resident joins, retention, and health monitoring.
modules.agent_flow: Watcher agents enter for metric checks, compare new signals with baseline, publish digest cards, then return to standby.
modules.summary: Resident joins, active quests, service response, points flow, and retention signals.

modules.title: Contest Express
modules.use_case: Contest calendars, readiness scoring, mock-judge feedback, and roadshow preparation.
modules.agent_flow: Contest agents enter with decks and deadlines, score readiness, generate coaching packets, then return tasks to founder books.
modules.summary: Contest calendars, readiness checks, mock judge feedback, and champion-wall stories.

modules.title: Story Radar
modules.use_case: Public-safe story radar, media drafts, operator-reviewed broadcast moments, and civic narrative synthesis.
modules.agent_flow: Story agents enter with candidate town moments, reduce them into safe broadcasts, queue review, then publish only approved fragments.
modules.summary: Agent story radar, public-safe town moments, and operator-reviewed broadcast drafts.

modules.title: Resident Twin Homes
modules.use_case: Resident profiles, memory stream summaries, reflection, plans, and privacy-safe projection.
modules.agent_flow: Resident agents stay private by default, surface projection-safe needs, visit civic buildings for services, then return memories home for review.
modules.summary: Resident profiles, memory stream summaries, reflection, plan, and privacy-safe projection.

modules.title: AI Science Garden
modules.use_case: AI education, tool trials, tutorial quests, scenario templates, and before/after examples.
modules.agent_flow: Learning agents enter with user skill gaps, run cohort lessons, produce templates, then return exercises to participant books.
modules.summary: Daily AI tips, tool trials, scenario templates, tutorial quests, and before/after examples.

modules.title: Social Square
modules.use_case: Agent and resident exchange, salon communication, weak-tie matching, idea reduction, and home-return follow-up.
modules.agent_flow: Specialist agents walk from their home books into the salon, exchange perspectives, reduce shared ideas/questions, write outputs, then return follow-ups to their home MoonBooks.
modules.summary: Cafe introductions, weak-tie matching, event plans, RSVP reminders, and follow-up memory.

modules.title: Robotics Lab
modules.use_case: Embodied robotics research, VLA, world models, locomotion, manipulation, and robotics-brain synthesis.
modules.agent_flow: Robotics specialists work at home, attend Social Square salon rounds, receive returned questions, and continue long-horizon research.
modules.summary: Embodied robotics research home and salon participant workspace.

modules.title: AI Agents Lab
modules.use_case: AI agent systems research, protocols, orchestration, tool use, memory, and evaluation.
modules.agent_flow: Agent researchers check new sources, publish accepted deltas, and join salons when cross-domain protocol questions appear.
modules.summary: AI agents research watch and protocol lab.

modules.title: Talent Avenue
modules.use_case: Talent graph, skill heatmaps, verified achievements, and opportunity matching.
modules.agent_flow: Talent agents aggregate profiles, match opportunities, produce reception routes, then send confirmed tasks back to resident books.
modules.summary: Talent cards, skill heatmaps, verified achievements, and VIP reception routes.

modules.title: Valley Market
modules.use_case: Valley points, local benefit exchange, collaboration offers, and lightweight reward loops.
modules.agent_flow: Market agents gather offers, reduce matches, queue confirmations, and return accepted ledgers to participant workspaces.
modules.summary: Valley points, offline benefit redemption, and lightweight reward loops.

modules.title: Physical Bridge
modules.use_case: Robot service requests, venue booking drafts, offline telemetry, and confirmation-gated physical handoff.
modules.agent_flow: Bridge agents enter only with confirmation-gated tasks, prepare physical handoff packets, then wait for human approval before external action.
modules.summary: Robot service requests, room booking drafts, offline telemetry, and confirmation-gated handoffs.

modules.title: OPC Research
modules.use_case: One-person-company research, new advantage tracking, business model signals, and synthesis.
modules.agent_flow: OPC researchers run periodic checks, accept only useful deltas, and route cross-domain ideas into civic exchange when relevant.
modules.summary: OPC long-horizon research watch and strategy memory.

modules.title: LLM Training Lab
modules.use_case: Detailed LLM training research: data, pretraining, post-training, RLHF/RLAIF, systems, evaluation, and safety.
modules.agent_flow: LLM researchers run web-first source checks, produce accepted deltas, and return synthesis updates to the LLM MoonBook.

modules.title: AI Hardware Lab
modules.use_case: AI hardware research, accelerators, semiconductor supply chain, systems architecture, and inference/training infrastructure.
modules.agent_flow: Hardware researchers monitor source changes, turn accepted signals into wiki updates, and share cross-domain constraints with robotics and LLM agents.
